name: Google Nest Learning Thermostat (4th Generation)

specs.Auto-Schedule: Learns your preferences automatically over 1 week
specs.Remote Sensing: Works with Nest Temperature Sensors (sold separately)

whatWorks: Auto-learning is the Nest Learning Thermostat's signature capability and it genuinely works — within one week, the thermostat observes your adjustments and builds a schedule that reflects when you're actually home and what temperatures you prefer. Most users report setting it once and never touching it again.

The 4th generation adds a full-color display that shows the current temperature, set point, and ambient conditions at a glance from across the room. The display also shows the current time and weather, making the thermostat useful as a room display rather than just a temperature control.

Energy History in the Nest app provides detailed heating and cooling usage breakdowns with a leaf icon when you hit energy-efficient temperatures. Google's estimates suggest 10–15% savings on heating and 15% on cooling — consistent with third-party studies showing meaningful energy reduction for users who weren't previously on programmable schedules.
Farsight technology detects when someone approaches and wakes the display to show the current temperature — a small detail that makes the interface feel polished and responsive rather than passive.
worthKnowing: The learning feature requires active use to work properly — the thermostat learns from manual adjustments. Users who rarely change temperatures (set once and leave it) won't see meaningful learning. The auto-schedule only improves when given behavioral data to learn from.

Nest Temperature Sensors extend remote sensing to multiple rooms but are sold separately at $49 CAD each. For homes with multiple zones or rooms that heat and cool unevenly, the sensor investment adds up quickly and was expected to be included at this price point.

A C-wire (common wire) is required for stable operation. If your HVAC system doesn't have a C-wire available, Nest includes a Power Connector adapter, but the installation is more involved and may require professional help in some wiring configurations.

At $329 CAD, this is significantly more expensive than the Google Nest Thermostat at around $149 CAD or the ecobee at around $229 CAD. The additional cost buys the color display and learning capability — features that are genuinely useful but not essential for all buyers.

commonComplaints.title: Temperature sensors sold separately…
commonComplaints.body: For a $329 thermostat, I expected at least one remote sensor to be included. ecobee includes one and costs $100 less. Feels like an oversight.

commonComplaints.title: Learning requires consistent behavior to work…
commonComplaints.body: If you work from home some days and not others, the learning gets confused. It took about three weeks before it settled into something useful for my irregular schedule.

commonComplaints.title: C-wire installation was complicated…
commonComplaints.body: My old house didn't have a C-wire. The Power Connector adapter works but the installation was confusing enough that I paid an HVAC tech $75 to do it. Add that to the $329 price.

commonComplaints.title: Expensive compared to ecobee…
commonComplaints.body: ecobee does everything this does plus includes a remote sensor for $100 less. I went Nest because I'm all Google, but the value proposition is hard to defend objectively.

verdict: The Google Nest Learning Thermostat 4th Gen is the most intuitive smart thermostat for people who don't want to program anything — it observes your habits and builds a schedule automatically. The color display, learning engine, and Google ecosystem integration are class-leading. The price premium over the ecobee and standard Nest Thermostat is real, and remote sensors being sold separately stings at this price point — but for Google-first households, it's the best thermostat available.

realBuyers.quote: I adjusted it twice on day one. By day five it was running the schedule I would have programmed myself. By day ten it was running a better schedule than I would have.
Energy report shows I cut my heating bill by 18% versus last winter. That's real money in a Canadian winter.
The color display actually looks good on the wall. Not a plastic box — it looks like something designed to be seen.
Works perfectly with my Nest Hub. I can see the temperature from the kitchen display and adjust it with my voice without crossing the room.

sources.title: Google Nest Learning Thermostat 4th Gen — Amazon.ca
Google Nest Learning Thermostat 4th Gen Review — The Verge

metaDescription: Google Nest Learning Thermostat 4th Gen review for Canada: auto-learning schedule, color display, energy savings. Honest on sensor cost and C-wire needs. ~$329 CAD.
introText: The Google Nest Learning Thermostat (4th Generation) automatically learns your temperature preferences and builds a schedule without any manual programming, now with a 24-bit color display. Available on Amazon.ca (ASIN B0BPVSSH1Y) at around $329 CAD.
